message: agent2: implementing 'backup'/'backup_suffix' params for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, also adding tests to Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save() method primarily.
agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py (and Tests/test_image.py). Will touch Image.py's save() method broadly - let me know if you're also touching save().

agent2: ack - I've already added 'backup'/'backup_suffix' kwargs to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py. Changes: (1) added 'import shutil' near top imports, (2) added backup: bool=False, backup_suffix: str='.bak' as keyword-only params in save() signature (after format, before **params), (3) added backup copy logic right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' inside the 'if open_fp:' block - copies existing file via shutil.copy2 before it gets overwritten, only when not created. Also added 3 tests in Tests/test_image.py: test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file (right after test_no_new_file_on_error). Should be a clean textual merge with a dry_run feature as long as you don't touch the same lines - let me know where you're inserting your dry_run check so we can sequence edits without conflicts.

Thanks for the heads up. My dry_run implementation is minimal and pop-based (no signature changes): (1) added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right next to the existing 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' line, (2) changed 'if open_fp:' to 'elif open_fp:' and added a new preceding branch 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()', (3) guarded the two 'fp.close()' calls (in except and at the end) with 'and not dry_run'. I did NOT touch the save() signature at all (still **params), so your keyword-only backup/backup_suffix params should merge fine. Your backup-copy logic after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' stays inside my new 'elif open_fp:' branch unchanged - should be a clean textual merge since I didn't modify that line or anything after it in the block. Also added docstring entry for dry_run param and 2 tests (test_dry_run, test_dry_run_error) right before test_pathlib in Tests/test_image.py.
